description_long: This object is a classic CRT computer monitor with a boxy, rectangular shape and a slightly protruding screen. The front features a flat glass display framed by a thick, light gray bezel, with the Windows 95 startup screen visible, suggesting a 1990s era design. The sides and rear are composed of solid, angular plastic panels that taper inward toward the back, forming a deep enclosure typical of cathode ray tube technology. The monitor sits atop a wide, circular black base that provides stability and a slight elevation. No buttons or ports are visible on the front, sides, or rear, giving the monitor a clean, utilitarian appearance. The only visible branding is the Windows 95 logo displayed on the screen, with no manufacturer logo present on the bezel or casing.
description: A vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick, light gray bezel, deep rear enclosure, and a wide black circular base. The screen displays the Windows 95 logo, and the design is clean and utilitarian.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat glass display, thick light gray bezel, and Windows 95 logo on the screen.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the deep, angular rear enclosure and the solid, unadorned plastic casing.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the monitor's tapered, boxy back and the wide circular base.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the depth and angularity of the rear casing.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and light gray with sharp corners and a smooth, matte plastic finish. It is uniform in color and lacks decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is a wide, circular pedestal base in black plastic, providing stable support without visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on the front, sides, or rear of the monitor.
